metadata.title: The Cancer Trial That Couldn't Diagnose Its Own Failure
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Cancer Nanomedicine card 19. Source chapter: cancer-nanomedicine/chapters/12-clinical-strategy-and-the-gap-book.md (response-only endpoint / three failure modes / delivery measurement only).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; hand-drawn annotation ring used once per graphic at most
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, TEAL = delivery confirmed / fix found / diagnosable result, CRIMSON = failure / ambiguity / unattributable negative, gold highlighter as single editor's-pen voice

metadata.color_semantics: TEAL #1F6F5C = the good/diagnosable outcome — a result that points to a fix; CRIMSON #BF3339 = the bad/undiagnosable outcome — a negative that cannot be attributed; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO companion diagnostic regulatory pathway, NO accelerated approval mechanism, NO three-arm trial design, NO specific assay protocols. One principle only: a response-only endpoint cannot tell you which of three failure modes caused the failure, so it cannot inform the next design decision. THE QUESTION appears on-screen in B03 as a CARD beat with the gap formula verbatim. Illustrative numbers in B11 (THE EXAMPLE) labeled as such in FACTCHECK. Key visual: the split/tree — one NEGATIVE result node at top branching into three labeled failure modes, CRIMSON split lines, TEAL fix arrows.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Pragmatist register (voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md + HAI.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the HAI outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Humanitarians AI
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: A targeted nanoparticle completes its cancer trial. Response rate: six percent — below standard of care. The program closes. The failure mode is unattributable.
beats.new_visual_element: [TITLE] the trial that couldn't diagnose its own failure

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [TITLE] the trial that couldn't diagnose its own failure

beats.narration_text: The particle: a polymeric nanoparticle with a targeting ligand, designed for tumor-targeted chemotherapy delivery. Preclinical data supported advancement. All-comers with the indicated tumor type enrolled.

props.headline: The particle was elegant — a polymeric nanoparticle with a targeting…

beats.scene_description: A schematic cross-section of a polymeric nanoparticle with targeting ligands on its surface and a small molecule payload inside — editorial flat diagram on aged newsprint background, teal outline, muted palette
beats.image_prompt: B02, schematic cross-section of a spherical polymeric nanoparticle, targeting ligands shown as small hook shapes on the outer surface, enclosed drug payload illustrated as small dots inside the core, flat editorial biomedical diagram style, aged newsprint background, desaturated muted palette, teal outline, no digital glow, documentary print reproduction
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL·ai] polymeric nanoparticle schematic — targeting ligand surface, payload core

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [STILL·ai] polymeric nanoparticle schematic — targeting ligand surface, payload core

beats.narration_text: A nanoparticle trial in cancer patients produces a negative result. Company leadership proposes switching to a more potent payload. Before spending eighty million dollars on that — what should the team have measured first, and why can't the response readout tell them?

card.copy: A nanoparticle trial in cancer patients produces a negative result. Company leadership proposes switching to a more potent payload. Before spending $80 million on that — what should the team have measured first, and why can't the response readout tell them?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] THE QUESTION — on screen verbatim

beats.narration_text: A response endpoint produces one bit of information: tumors shrank, or they did not. That binary signal carries no information about mechanism. Everything about what happened inside the trial is invisible to it.

production_viz.label: response endpoint: worked / didn't
production_viz.mechanic: a single large box at center labeled RESPONSE ENDPOINT in SLATE; two arms fork left and right: left arm leads to a TEAL chip WORKED, right arm leads to a CRIMSON chip DIDN'T. Labels ONLY — no further branching yet. Clean binary.
production_viz.note: establish the binary endpoint before revealing what it cannot see

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] binary endpoint: worked vs didn't — two arms from one box

beats.narration_text: A negative response is identical regardless of cause. The trial reports it did not work. It cannot report which step failed. The result is unattributable.

document.quote: The result cannot be attributed to delivery failure, payload failure, or biology failure.

beats.scene_description: large serif quote card; gold highlighter sweeps 'cannot be attributed' as spoken
beats.new_visual_element: [DOCUMENT] quote: result cannot be attributed to any failure mode

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [DOCUMENT] quote: result cannot be attributed to any failure mode

beats.narration_text: There are exactly three mechanistically distinct ways a nanoparticle trial can produce a negative result. All three are collapsed into the same single readout.

production_viz.label: one NEGATIVE, three failure roots
production_viz.mechanic: a CRIMSON box at top labeled NEGATIVE RESPONSE. Three CRIMSON lines split downward to three labeled boxes: DELIVERY FAILURE (left), PAYLOAD FAILURE (center), BIOLOGY FAILURE (right). No fix arrows yet — just the split. This is the key split/tree visual.
production_viz.note: the split move — CRIMSON for the split lines. Three branches emerge from one result. No TEAL yet.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the failure tree: one NEGATIVE node splits to three failure modes — CRIMSON branches

beats.narration_text: Failure mode one: the particle never reached the tumor. Immune clearance, sequestration in liver and spleen, or inability to extravasate into tumor tissue. The drug was absent from the target site.

production_viz.label: failure mode 1: delivery failure
production_viz.mechanic: the DELIVERY FAILURE branch from B06 is highlighted CRIMSON; a particle path diagram shows: particle injected, particle diverts to liver (crimson arrow to LIVER/SPLEEN chip), tumor shown with no signal. Label: PARTICLE NEVER ARRIVED. Then below a TEAL arrow pointing right labeled FIX: redesign the particle.
production_viz.note: focus on one branch; TEAL fix arrow introduced

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] delivery failure branch: particle path to liver, not tumor — fix arrow in TEAL

beats.narration_text: Failure mode two: the particle arrived at the tumor, but the payload released in the wrong location — into circulation, before reaching tumor cells, or after clearance. Drug released; tumor cells were not exposed at therapeutic concentrations.

production_viz.label: failure mode 2: payload failure
production_viz.mechanic: PAYLOAD FAILURE branch highlighted CRIMSON. Schematic shows particle arriving at tumor (teal line to tumor outline), then premature release shown as a burst outside the tumor cell (crimson burst symbol). Label: PAYLOAD RELEASED EARLY. TEAL fix arrow: FIX: redesign the release mechanism.
production_viz.note: delivery path is TEAL (particle arrived); release problem is CRIMSON (burst in wrong location)

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] payload failure branch: particle arrives, premature release — fix arrow in TEAL

beats.narration_text: Failure mode three: delivery worked, release worked — the cancer cells did not respond. Wrong target, acquired resistance, or a patient population whose tumor biology was outside the design assumptions.

production_viz.label: failure mode 3: biology failure
production_viz.mechanic: BIOLOGY FAILURE branch highlighted CRIMSON. Particle path to tumor in TEAL, release in tumor in TEAL, but tumor cells show no response (crimson X on tumor cell). Label: TUMOR DID NOT RESPOND. TEAL fix arrow: FIX: try a different target.
production_viz.note: delivery chain is TEAL (it worked); biology outcome is CRIMSON (X on cell)

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] biology failure branch: particle delivered, cell unresponsive — fix arrow in TEAL

beats.narration_text: Three failure modes. Three different fixes. A response-only readout produces the same negative signal for all three. Without built-in delivery measurement, the team cannot distinguish them — and the next design decision has no informational basis.

production_viz.label: full failure tree with fix arrows
production_viz.mechanic: the complete tree from B06 shown: CRIMSON NEGATIVE box at top, three CRIMSON split lines to three branches; each branch now has a TEAL fix arrow pointing downward: DELIVERY FAILURE -> FIX THE PARTICLE, PAYLOAD FAILURE -> FIX THE RELEASE, BIOLOGY FAILURE -> TRY A DIFFERENT TARGET. Response-only readout shown as a CRIMSON block across the top labeled RESPONSE ONLY — CANNOT SEE BELOW THIS LINE.
production_viz.note: the complete split/tree: CRIMSON split lines for failures, TEAL fix arrows — the key visual of the reel

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] full failure tree: CRIMSON branches, TEAL fix arrows — cannot see below response line

beats.narration_text: Adding delivery measurement to the trial — an imaging tracer cohort, a labeled particle variant — converts a binary negative into a diagnosable result. The program identifies the failure mode and has a basis for the next design iteration.

props.headline: Building delivery measurement into the trial — an imaging tracer cohort,…

beats.scene_description: A schematic showing a small cohort of patient silhouettes with imaging scan overlays above them — editorial newsprint diagram showing PET/fluorescence overlay on patient outline, warm desaturated tones, tracer cohort concept
beats.image_prompt: B11, editorial biomedical schematic of a small group of five patient silhouettes arranged in a row, above each silhouette a simplified PET scan cross-section showing tracer distribution in body — a biodistribution tracer cohort illustration, aged newsprint background, flat documentary diagram style, muted warm tones, no digital glow
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL·ai] tracer cohort schematic — patient silhouettes with scan overlays

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [STILL·ai] tracer cohort schematic — patient silhouettes with scan overlays

beats.narration_text: Illustrative comparison: two programs, identical nanoparticles, ovarian cancer. Program A measures response only — seven percent response, program closed. Program B runs a ten-patient biodistribution cohort before efficacy. Imaging shows more than seventy-five percent of tracer in liver at four hours, less than three percent in tumor. Delivery failure diagnosed. PEG coating redesigned. Next efficacy trial: twenty-one percent response. Same particle class. Program B had information to act on.

production_viz.label: two programs, same particle — illustrative
production_viz.mechanic: two side-by-side columns. Left PROGRAM A: CRIMSON box 7% RESPONSE, large CRIMSON chip CLOSED. Right PROGRAM B: top chip TRACER COHORT FIRST (10 patients), then small bar chart (LIVER 75%+ crimson bar vs TUMOR less than 3% teal bar), chip DELIVERY FAILURE DIAGNOSED, chip REDESIGN PEG, chip 21% RESPONSE in TEAL. Illustrative label at top of scene.
production_viz.note: ALL numbers (7%, 75%, 3%, 21%, 10 patients) are illustrative — labeled as such in FACTCHECK. This is THE EXAMPLE act, 16:9 full cut only.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] two program columns: A = closed at 7%, B = diagnosed + redesigned + 21% — illustrative

beats.narration_text: A response-only endpoint cannot distinguish delivery failure, payload failure, or biology failure. It terminates programs without attributing causes. Measure delivery within the trial — and a negative result becomes actionable information for the next design.

card.copy: A negative result is only informative if the trial was designed to diagnose it.

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] endcard — diagnosable negative vs unattributable negative

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] endcard — diagnosable negative vs unattributable negative
